title: cordylanthus rigidus subsp. rigidus
intro: Cordylanthus rigidus subsp. rigidus, also known as the stiffleaf bird's beak, is a perennial herb native to the western coast of North America, belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of stiffleaf bird's beak include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Cordylanthus rigidus subsp. rigidus often involve drying the leaves and flowers to make teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
